Data sources

Arbitrum on-chain data Internal EA db
Block headers, tx counts, base fees, L1 calldata costs and reverts. Wide window. Drives the observed-on-chain reference series, the ArbOS 51 baseline and the spam-classification flags.
Blocks Transactions Reverted transactions
Per-tx multi-gas extracts Offchain Labs node extracts
Per-transaction breakdown of every priced resource (computation, storage read/write/growth, history growth, L2/L1 calldata, WASM compute). Drives the ArbOS 60 + per- resource backlog panels.
Blocks Transactions (with per-resource gas)

Day type, before vs after ArbOS DIA
Congested = at least 5% of the day’s L2 fees came from surplus pricing (price above p_min). Otherwise the day is normal.

Hourly priced gas: absolute and composition

Top: Mgas/hr by resource. Bottom: same data, normalised to 100 %.

Per-transaction gas distribution

Distribution of per-transaction gas usage across the seven priced resources, computed on a uniform 500,000-tx sample drawn from the full 594,056,892-tx pool. The % zero column shows the share of transactions that consume none of that resource. All values are in raw gas units.
